name: Are Johns Hopkins' Engineering Innovation Precollege Programs Worth It?
overnight_evidence_snippet: Johns Hopkins University's Engineering Innovation programs are a set of four-week pre-college programs that aim to inspire and educate high school students in the field of engineering . They are designed to provide a comprehensive introduction to engineering concepts through hands-on projects, lab work, and lectures.

name: Engineering Innovation - summerapply.com
overnight_evidence_snippet: Explore Engineering Innovation (EEI), formerly called Engineering Innovation , is an exciting, college-level, summer program for motivated high school students with an aptitude in math and science and an interest in (or curiosity about) engineering .

name: Explore Engineering with Johns Hopkins University!
overnight_evidence_snippet: Johns Hopkins University's Engineering Innovation Pre-College Programs offer high school students like you the chance to explore your passion for math, science, and technology while experiencing college life. Whether you choose to live on campus, commute from home, or participate online, these programs are designed to help you see your future as an engineer.
